plugins.name: nav
plugins.description: Keep any codebase navigable as it grows — audit (assess + feasibility + deep legacy sweep via domain fan-out), refactor (verbatim move + test gates), sync (file-top headers), map (bilingual codebase HTML), plan (spec → grounded plan), do (execute a small behaviour-changing change, disciplined, no artifact), compose (author/restructure a prose document as a deep module). Language-agnostic. Built on Ousterhout's deep-module principles (deep modules via information hiding).

plugins.name: shape
plugins.description: Forward motion: give intent its form via real disposable instances (not descriptions), record decisions legibly, keep them current, and build them into running, verified code. Sibling to nav. Skills: mockup (render-to-decide), elicit (grounded grill → a thought), position (canon-authoring campaign → core docs), setup (scaffold a new project to a verified baseline — archetype references + stack principles), dogfood (use a built feature for real → friction + coverage gaps), align (decide next + render the plan), reconcile (prune stale notes), build (drive the plan's In-progress to done); doctor deferred. Language-agnostic.

plugins.name: research
plugins.description: Rigorous reading and auditing of argument documents — papers, RFCs, design proposals, ADRs, whitepapers (a paper is the most common instance, not the definition). Dissect one or more documents into Gap/Claim/Mechanism/Evidence skeletons, untangle how a set of documents relate (lineage / clusters / contradictions / contested ground, with optional claim-framed positioning), critique a document adversarially into a referee report (claim↔evidence audit + self-attack), or run a provenance audit over your own documents (trace every load-bearing citation back to a verified source; quarantine the unread).

plugins.name: frame
plugins.description: Apply an explicit, named frame that forces a structure the default skips — to a problem (four reasoning lenses: first-principles, orthogonal, dialectic, graft) or to an answer you already have (analogize — build a stress-tested analogy so it lands in plain language). Lenses feed shape one-way; analogize does not. Standalone. Renamed from think.

plugins.name: reflect
plugins.description: Reflect on your own working session — the one reflexive, cross-cutting family. Four summoned skills: catchup/park work the cursor, observe distills selected durable learning, and retrace reconstructs a long development arc as evidence-backed causal stages before rendering a user-corrected interactive alignment artifact. A plain chronological recap remains skill-free. Read-only except each writer's bounded artifact. Cross-project, standalone.

plugins.name: relay
plugins.description: Coordinate asynchronously through GitHub-native Discussions, Issues, pull requests, reviews, and protected merges. Six daily verbs — launch, report, digest, reply, brief, settle — route intent, expose obligations, preserve reusable understanding, and close work with authority. An explicit migrate bridge preserves legacy provenance. GitHub owns collaboration state; Relay owns semantics and verification. Language-agnostic.
